text:
MOULTRIE, Ga. (WALB) - It is a day of excitement in South Georgia where Moultrie native Carson Tyler competed for a gold medal on Thursday morning in the Men’s 3-meter Diving event.

He finished in 4th place overall with 429.25 points, so it’s safe to say he’s one of the best divers in the world.

Moultrie Mayor William McIntosh described Thursday’s historic event with one word: “Wow.”

Tyler is making history with his Olympic debut being the first American since 2000 to qualify for the Olympic Games on both the 3-meter and 10-meter in the same year.

It is truly incredible, especially coming from a small town here in South Georgia. Tyler’s family and friends have one message for the people of South Georgia, “Never give up on your dreams because you can definitely make it.”

Carson’s family tells WALB that the 10-meter is actually his favorite dive, so they’re super excited to see what Carson Tyler can bring to the table on the world stage.

“Moose Moss, who started this program that Carson is a part of in this article said, ‘Maybe one of these days, my dream is to have an Olympic diver and here we are today. It came true.’” McIntosh said.

Carson Tyler, from a young age, knew what he wanted to do, would get up early in the morning, come to the YMCA in Moultrie, hop up on this diving board, dive into this pool and eventually years later, launch him to the world stage.

“We have so many younger ones that have been watching him and being the ones that want to get up early in the morning and see Carson dive off that platform so what an amazing opportunity to have one of our own out there,” said Executive Director of Moultrie YMCA Jennifer Buckner.

“What he’s thinking about is the next time. Because he will do better the next time, I mean, that’s just how he does, next time you will see him get a medal.” Glenda Bozeman, Tyler’s grandmother, said.

Downtown Moultrie is layered with images of support toward Tyler.

You can see that Olympic Pride and that support for him So many people have “Go for Gold,” “Paris 2024,” “Olympic 2024,” “Carson Tyler.” His name is known in Moultrie, and it just goes to show the support that he has from his hometown right here in South Georgia.

“We’re a very close-knit community so everybody wants to come and support someone that’s in such a dynamic situation of being in the Olympics, we’re super proud of him,” said supporter Jackie Miles.

Tyler’s grandparents watched with pride in their eyes as they saw him do an amazing performance.

“It has been very emotional just to get to see him participate because it has been his lifelong dream to do this and we are so proud of him and we wish him the very best of luck on the 10M,” said Patricia Tyler, Carson’s grandmother.

“So really the community was calling for this, saying they wanted to come together as a group, as a community and support Carson and cheer him on,” Executive Director of Moultrie CVB Caroline Barber said.

”I’m trying real hard to hold back the tears because I’m so proud of him.“

”To see somebody jump like that, dive like that and I dive like a rock and swim like a feather, and I just go to the bottom and stay forever,” Linda and Bobby Wiard, Tyler’s great uncle and aunt, said.

”Keep up the good work and God bless you, and we love you very much,” Dianne and Dorsey Wiard, Tyler’s great uncle and great aunt, said.

The 10-meter preliminary platform competition is on Friday. Aug. 9 at 4 a.m. and Saturday, Aug. 10 at 9 a.m. is the 10-meter finals.

To participate in the watch party, head to the Welcome Center in Downtown Moultrie on August 10th at 9 a.m., and on Aug. 22 there will be a welcome home parade for Tyler.

title: Moultrie native Carson Tyler finishes 4th in the 3-meter Diving Olympic Finals
description: The mayor of Moultrie, William McIntosh, described today’s historic event with one word, ‘wow’.
